creator: 000.fbsd@quip.cz
text:
Messages like this are useful only when they are new, "unexpected" and if there are only few of them. If you read these messages every day for 2 years, they are useless spam masking other useful information.

If I apply your logic about "more predicable" to the latest PHP branch 8.3, than it should be marked as EXPIRATION_DATE 2027-12-31 - it is accurate and predictable. 
But I don't think it is useful to print messages about it now. Nor for PHP 8.3, nor for 8.1, nor for 8.2. They all will expire too far in the future. 
The less noise, the better, should be applied here.
